name: Coda Doc API
what_it_does: All-in-one document and database platform with REST API — combining rich text documents with structured tables and automations, enabling teams to build custom apps, wikis, and project management tools, with API for reading and writing doc content programmatically.
use_cases: Reading and writing table rows in Coda docs programmatically for data integration
Creating and updating documents via API for automated report generation
Syncing external database records into Coda tables for team dashboards
Triggering Coda automations from external events via API
Exporting Coda table data to downstream systems for analytics pipelines
not_for: Pure database use without document context (use Airtable or Notion)
Heavy computational data processing (use dbt or Python notebooks)
Traditional CMS without structured data needs (use Contentful or WordPress)
best_when: A team uses Coda as their primary workspace and needs to automate data entry, sync external data into tables, or export Coda data to other systems.
avoid_when: You need a pure database API, complex relational queries, or don't already use Coda as your workspace.

auth.notes: API key from account settings. Bearer token in Authorization header. Account-scoped access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Single API key per account with access to all owned docs.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited docs, 1,000 rows per doc
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $10/user/month; Team from $30/user/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: API access available on all plans including free. Row limit on free tier may constrain large-scale data use. US-based. Strong enterprise adoption. Good JavaScript and Python SDKs. Packs ecosystem for integrations.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covering docs, pages, tables, rows, and columns. JavaScript/Python SDKs available. Good documentation with examples. No webhooks — polling required for change detection. Rate limit of 100 req/min well-documented. Row and table IDs required for operations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Row upsert by key column value — idempotent with upsert flag. Row creation without upsert creates duplicate. Doc and page management idempotent. Column management idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: No webhooks — must poll for table changes; adds latency to event-driven workflows
Doc and table IDs are opaque strings — must fetch from list endpoints first
Row upsert requires keyColumns parameter — not default behavior
Rate limit of 100 req/min — bulk operations need throttling

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. API key auth. US data residency. No fine-grained scopes — full account access with single key. Standard SaaS security. No OAuth2 for user-level access control.
